channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New top prosecutor for DC advocated for Jan. 6 rioters and echoed Trump's false 2020 election claims
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For years, conservative activist Ed Martin has promoted Donald Trump's false claims about a stolen 2020 election, railed against the prosecution of the rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol and represented some of them in court.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan Democratic Sen. Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening a key Senate seat in 2026
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Sen. Gary Peters, who led the Democrats’ Senate campaign efforts the past two election cycles, has announced he will not seek a third term in 2026, creating a highly contested battleground seat expected to be coveted by both major political parties.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal battle looms as Trump orders a funding freeze during a review of federal loans and grants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House is pausing federal grants and loans starting Tuesday as President Donald Trump's administration begins an across-the-board ideological review of its spending, causing confusion and panic among organizations that rely on Washington for their financial lifeline.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate poised to approve Trump Transportation Department pick Sean Duffy
item.description: Sean Duffy previously told lawmakers his priorities leading the Transportation Department include aviation and highway safety as well as addressing the air traffic controller shortage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s nominee for Pentagon weapons buyer was a key figure in the president's first impeachment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to be the military's top weapons buyer is an official who directed the Pentagon to withhold aid from Ukraine in 2019 as Trump sought a commitment from President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to investigate the Biden family — a key component of the impeachment
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance to address House Republicans at Trump hotel amid division over budget bill
item.description: House Republicans are set to hear from Vice President JD Vance on Tuesday as they work to chart a path forward on plans for a massive conservative policy overhaul.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump orders a funding freeze as his administration reviews federal loans and grants
item.description: The White House is pausing federal grants and loans starting on Tuesday as President Donald Trump's administration begins an across-the-board ideological review of its spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu hopes to meet Trump in Washington as soon as next week, US officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is hoping to meet with President Donald Trump in Washington as early as next week, according to two U.S. officials familiar with preliminary planning for the trip.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pushes border crackdown at House Republican retreat as he caps first week in office
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump regaled House Republicans on Monday with a rhetorical highlight reel from his first week in office, and he urged them to support his immigration crackdown and border security proposals. “We have no apologies and we’re moving very fast," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Bessent as Trump's Treasury secretary
item.description: Scott Bessent on Monday secured confirmation to lead the Department of the Treasury, putting President Trump’s top economic nominee in place ahead of a daunting GOP push to enact Trump's ambitious economic agenda. Senators voted 68-29 on Bessent’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Johnson opens door to linking voter ID to California disaster aid after Trump demand
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is keeping the door open to conditioning California disaster aid on the state enacting voter identification laws, days after President Trump said he wanted to link the two priorities.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bishop who enraged Trump says she was speaking from faith
item.description: Trump, who sat alongside JD Vance, appeared annoyed, looking away from Budde as she spoke directly to him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: A CDC official sent a memo to senior leaders at the agency on Sunday night telling them that all agency staff who work with the WHO must immediately stop their collaborations and “await further guidance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vance political advisers join Ramaswamy ahead of expected Ohio governor run
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy is set to launch his bid mid-February, per the source familiar, to succeed term-limited Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: New Trump orders on transgender troops, COVID and more expected on Hegseth’s first day
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will sign a flurry of executive orders focused on the military, including to reinstate troops booted for refusing COVID-19 vaccines and remove “transgender ideology” from the force.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's celebration of American greatness puts a spotlight on a little-known panel of experts
item.description: Within hours of taking office, President Donald Trump outlined in one of his many executive orders a mission to celebrate American greatness and to recognize those who have made contributions throughout history.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Trump's embattled pick for defense secretary, wins Senate confirmation
item.description: Vice President JD Vance cast a tie-breaking vote as Hegseth overcame allegations of sexual assault, public drunkenness and questions of financial mismanagement to win Senate approval.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
